info.title: GoClaw Gateway API
info.description:
PostgreSQL multi-tenant AI agent gateway with WebSocket RPC + HTTP API.

## Authentication

All endpoints require a Bearer token in the `Authorization` header:

```
Authorization: Bearer <gateway-token-or-api-key>
```

You can use either the **gateway token** (grants admin access) or an **API key** created via the API Keys endpoints (grants scoped access).

If no gateway token is configured, empty-token admin access is limited to loopback local development or explicit `GOCLAW_ALLOW_INSECURE_NO_AUTH=1`; externally reachable deployments require `GOCLAW_GATEWAY_TOKEN`.

## Common Headers

| Header | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| `X-GoClaw-User-Id` | External user ID for multi-tenant context |
| `X-GoClaw-Agent-Id` | Target agent ID (alternative to model prefix) |
| `X-GoClaw-Tenant-Id` | Tenant scope — UUID or slug (gateway token / cross-tenant API keys) |
| `Accept-Language` | Locale for error messages (`en`, `vi`, `zh`) |

## WebSocket Protocol

Connect via `POST /ws` (upgrade). Protocol v3 uses frame types: `req`, `res`, `event`.
First request must be `connect` with `{"token": "...", "user_id": "...", "locale": "en"}`.

tags.name: OAuth
tags.description: Provider-scoped OAuth status and quota endpoints

tags.name: Vault
tags.description: Knowledge Vault: documents, links, search, tree, upload, enrichment

tags.name: Vault Graph
tags.description: Lightweight graph view of vault documents + links for visualization

post.description: Stores explicitly selected cookies for the authenticated user and one agent. Cookie values are encrypted at rest and are never returned by list APIs.

parameters.name: X-GoClaw-User-Id
parameters.description: Authenticated external user ID for cookie scope

expirationDate.description: Unix seconds as returned by chrome.cookies

get.description: Lists metadata for cookies scoped to the authenticated user and requested agent. Cookie values are redacted.

post.description:
Send messages to an agent and get a response. Compatible with OpenAI's chat completions API.

Use the `model` field to target a specific agent: `goclaw:<agent-id>` or `agent:<agent-id>`.

200.description: Chat completion response (or SSE stream if stream=true)

get.summary: Poll chat session history by index cursor

get.description: Returns all API keys (active and revoked). Key hashes are never included in responses. Requires admin access.

post.description:
Creates a new API key with the specified scopes. The raw key is returned **only once** in the response — store it securely.

Available scopes:
- `operator.admin` — Full admin access
- `operator.read` — Read-only access
- `operator.write` — Read + write access
- `operator.approvals` — Manage exec approvals
- `operator.pairing` — Manage device pairing

expires_in.description: Expiry in seconds. Omit or 0 for never.

201.description: API key created. The `key` field is shown only once.

get.summary: Fetch OpenAI Codex OAuth quota for a provider
get.description: Returns structured Codex/OpenAI quota state for a specific OpenAI Codex OAuth (`chatgpt_oauth`) provider. Missing provider returns `404`, provider type conflicts return `409`, and upstream quota problems return `200` with `success=false` so dashboards can render inline action states.

parameters.name: provider
parameters.description: Provider alias (for example `openai-codex` or `codex-work`).

get.summary: Fetch quota for the default OpenAI Codex OAuth provider

get.description: List all agents accessible to the current user. Owners see all agents.

post.description: Create a new agent with the specified configuration.

get.summary: Summarize recent OpenAI Codex OAuth pool activity for an agent
get.description: Returns explicit router-backed evidence for recent OpenAI Codex OAuth calls in the configured provider pool. Direct router selections are reported separately from failover serves, and each recent request shows both the selected alias and the alias that actually served the call.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of recent routed Codex LLM calls to include.

stats_sample_size.description: Number of recent routed llm_call spans used to derive runtime health. The server samples max(limit, 120) spans even when recent_requests remains capped by limit.

provider_name.description: Provider alias in the configured pool.

request_count.description: Backward-compatible count of requests where this alias was directly selected by the router.

direct_selection_count.description: Number of recent requests where the router selected this alias first.

failover_serve_count.description: Number of recent requests this alias served after another alias was selected first.

success_count.description: Trace-backed successes attributed to the alias that actually served the request.

failure_count.description: Trace-backed failures attributed to earlier attempted aliases on successful failover, or to every attempted alias on terminal error.

consecutive_failures.description: Newest-first failure streak for this alias.

success_rate.description: Success rate percent derived from recent runtime outcomes.

health_score.description: Heuristic runtime health score from 0 to 100.

health_state.description: Health band derived from recent runtime outcomes: idle when there are no outcomes, critical at 3+ consecutive failures or score < 40, degraded below 80, otherwise healthy.

provider_name.description: Alias that actually served the request. Empty when a terminal failure completed without any alias serving successfully.

selected_provider.description: Alias chosen first by the router before any failover.

post.description: Trigger an agent run from an external webhook or automation.
Reload an existing provider from stored config into the runtime registry. Does not mutate stored config and does not call upstream verification.

verify.description: Only false is supported. Call /v1/providers/{id}/verify separately for upstream verification.

200.description: Model list with optional reasoning capability metadata

reasoning_defaults.description: Provider-owned default reasoning policy returned when the provider has saved defaults and at least one discovered model exposes reasoning capability metadata.

post.description: Empty body or omitted model = ping mode (provider registered/reachable check). With model = full chat-verify against the upstream provider.

model.description: Optional. Omit (or send empty body) for ping mode.

get.description: Stateless polling endpoint for traces in one session or one agent. Requires session_key or agent_id.

get.summary: Aggregate recent runtime logs from the in-memory ring buffer

parameters.name: path
parameters.description: Subdirectory path

post.description: Multipart upload to the tenant workspace.

parameters.name: X-GoClaw-User-Id
parameters.description: External user ID for multi-tenant context.

200.description: Upload result with temp path and MIME type

get.description: List vault documents across all agents in the tenant. Non-owners see only personal + their teams' docs.

parameters.description: External user ID for multi-tenant context.

parameters.name: agent_id
parameters.description: Optional filter by agent.

parameters.name: scope
parameters.description: Filter by scope. Owner sees all by default; non-owners are restricted to personal + their teams.

parameters.name: doc_type
parameters.description: Comma-separated list of doc_types.

post.description: Create a vault document not bound to a specific agent. When no `team_id` is given the document is shared scope.

post.description: Accept multipart file uploads (up to 50 files, 50 MB each). Files are written to the tenant workspace under `agents/{agent_key}/`, `teams/{team_id}/`, or shared root, depending on form fields. Allowed extensions: md/txt/json/yaml/yml/csv/toml/xml/html/htm/go/py/js/ts/tsx/jsx/rs/java/rb/sh/sql/swift/kt/c/cpp/h.

parameters.name: X-GoClaw-User-Id
parameters.description: External user ID for multi-tenant context.

200.description: Upload result (some files may have errored individually).

post.description: Walk the tenant workspace and register missing/changed files. Per-tenant concurrency guard (returns 409 if another rescan is in progress).

parameters.name: X-GoClaw-User-Id
parameters.description: External user ID for multi-tenant context.

get.description: Return immediate children (files + virtual folders) under a path prefix for lazy-loading the vault sidebar.

parameters.name: X-GoClaw-User-Id
parameters.description: External user ID for multi-tenant context.

parameters.name: path
parameters.description: Path prefix to expand. Empty = root. Must not contain `..` or start with `/`.

parameters.name: doc_type
parameters.description: Comma-separated list of doc_types.

post.description: Hybrid full-text + vector search across the tenant. Pass `agent_id` in the body to restrict to one agent.

parameters.name: X-GoClaw-User-Id
parameters.description: External user ID for multi-tenant context.

agent_id.description: Only used by the tenant-wide endpoint. Ignored when the path already carries `{agentID}`.

chat_id.description: Optional. When set with team_id, restrict to same-chat + team-wide docs (isolated semantics).

get.description: Returns current per-tenant enrichment progress (summary generation + embeddings). May return an empty object if no enrichment is running or the worker is disabled.

503.description: Enrichment worker not available in this build.

get.description: Reads a file in the calling agent's workspace (or, with admin, any path). Used by the web UI's file browser.

parameters.name: X-GoClaw-User-Id
parameters.description: External user ID for multi-tenant context.

parameters.name: path
parameters.description: Workspace-relative path (`{path...}` route — slashes allowed).

post.summary: Sign a file URL for temporary public access

other_config.description: Optional legacy/extensibility bag. Do not nest reasoning or Codex routing here in new writes.

reasoning_config.description: Capability-aware reasoning policy for GPT-5/Codex models.

chatgpt_oauth_routing.description: Optional agent-side routing override for ChatGPT OAuth providers. The main provider field remains the preferred/default account, while provider settings may supply inherited defaults.

settings.description: Provider-specific settings such as provider-owned Codex pool defaults and reasoning defaults.

VaultDocument.description: Document registered in the Knowledge Vault. Content lives on disk in the tenant workspace; the registry holds metadata + summary + embedding + links.

agent_id.description: Owning agent. Null for team or shared documents.

chat_id.description: Set only in isolated teams to scope a document to one chat.

content_hash.description: SHA-256 hex digest of the file.

path.description: Workspace-relative path. Must not contain `..` or start with `/`.

team_id.description: When set, the request must be made by a member of the team. Implies scope=team.

VaultDocumentUpdateInput.description: Partial update. Omitted fields are left unchanged. `team_id`: nil = no change, `""` = clear (back to personal), uuid = move to team (owner only).

VaultLink.description: Directed link between two vault documents (wikilink, reference, attachment, ...).

context.description: Surrounding text snippet or source reference.

VaultBacklink.description: Enriched backlink: source doc metadata joined with the link row.

VaultTreeEntry.description: A file or virtual folder under a tree path.

VaultGraphNode.description: Lightweight vault doc for graph visualization. Field names are intentionally short to keep wire size small.

KGEntity.description: Knowledge Graph entity. `external_id` is the caller-supplied stable identifier; `id` is the server-assigned UUID.

CLICredential.description: Stored authentication binary preset for a CLI provider (claude-cli, codex, etc).
